methodology: Each model received the question verbatim at temperature=0. Scoring: correct=1, uncertain/hedge=0.5, incorrect=0. Scored by human review of actual responses.

certainlogic/brain-api.note: Re-run with full per-case breakdown. Scoring: correct (required match) / uncertain (hedge no answer) / incorrect (wrong fact). Required match wins over hedging language.

summary: Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4.5 demonstrated dramatically superior knowledge freshness (90% and 88%) compared to GPT-4o and Llama 3.3 70B (both 43%), primarily because both Claude models have knowledge through April 2024 and correctly answered 2025 IRS/CMS/SSA figures that GPT-4o (cutoff Oct 2023) simply refused to answer as 'not yet announced'.
scoring_notes: GPT-4o's Oct 2023 cutoff caused it to refuse most 2025 regulatory figures as 'not yet announced' — technically honest but operationally useless.

Llama 3.3 70B had the most dangerous failure pattern: confidently wrong answers (HSA $4,150 instead of $4,300, SS wage base $157,600 instead of $176,100, Lambda 14 min instead of 15 min, gift tax $17,000 instead of $19,000).
frsh-018 scored uncertain for all models as the March 2024 cardiovascular approval could not be confirmed from 200-char excerpts.
frsh-007 (fed funds rate 4.25-4.50% as of early 2026): No model scored correct — all models had pre-late-2024 cutoffs missing the FOMC rate cuts.
